strengths: Completed the full circle cutting task within reasonable time
Maintained steady traction throughout most of the cutting process
Successfully achieved circle release and final separation
weaknesses: Line deviation occurred during the cutting process inside the marked circle
Inconsistent precision when following the circular path
Could improve smoothness of scissor movement around curves

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between each quarter to reset hand position and verify alignment with the marked line.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle, focusing on maintaining optimal cutting geometry throughout curved sections.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent precision when following the circular path.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion ability into cleaner cuts with minimal deviations. Your time is competitive, so prioritize precision refinement over speed.
